title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement sbudai.ga@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. what: Look for the Polish market EIC and set the start and the end of scope dates
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette
code: pl_eic <- all_approved_eic() |>   filter(EicLongName == "Poland") |>   select("EicCode") |>   unlist() #> ℹ downloading X_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading Y_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading Z_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading T_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading V_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading W_eicCodes.csv file ... #> ℹ downloading A_eicCodes.csv file ...  from_ts <- force_tz(time = as.Date("2026-01-01"), tzone = "CET") till_ts <- from_ts + weeks(x = 1L)  cli_inform("Polish EIC: '{pl_eic}'") #> Polish EIC: '10YPL-AREA-----S' cli_inform("from: {from_ts}") #> from: 2026-01-01 cli_inform("till: {till_ts}") #> till: 2026-01-08

what: Query the Polish DA prices within the pre-set period
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette

what: Calculate the daily minimum and maximum prices and the spread
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette
code: da_spreads <- da_prices |>   select(c(ts_point_dt_start, ts_point_price_amount)) |>   mutate(     ts_point_dt_start = with_tz(time = ts_point_dt_start, tzone = "CET")   ) |>   mutate(ts_point_date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")) |>   group_by(ts_point_date) |>   mutate(     max_price = max(ts_point_price_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     min_price = min(ts_point_price_amount, na.rm = TRUE)   ) |>   mutate(     price_spread = max_price - min_price   ) |>   select(c(ts_point_date, min_price, max_price, price_spread)) |>   unique()

what: Plot the daily minimum and maximum prices and the spread
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette
code: ggplot(data = da_spreads) +   geom_segment(     mapping = aes(       x = ts_point_date,       xend = ts_point_date,       y = min_price,       yend = max_price     ),     linewidth = 10,     color = "tomato"   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(       x = ts_point_date,       y = max_price + 10,       label = price_spread     ),     size = 4   ) +   scale_x_date(     breaks = da_spreads$ts_point_date,     date_labels = "%Y-%m-%d"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Polish Daily Spread of Day-Ahead prices",     subtitle = paste("PT15M,", from_ts, till_ts),     x = "delivery date",     y = "day-ahead price spread"   ) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)   )

what: Get Elastic Demands (IFs aFRR 3.4 & mFRR 3.4) — elastic_demands
title: Get Elastic Demands (IFs aFRR 3.4 & mFRR 3.4) — elastic_demands
text: Elastic demands scheduled activation standard aFRR/mFRR product.

title: Get Elastic Demands (IFs aFRR 3.4 & mFRR 3.4) — elastic_demands
code: elastic_demands(   eic = NULL,   process_type = NULL,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() - lubridate::days(x = 7L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date(), tz = "CET"),   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Elastic Demands (IFs aFRR 3.4 & mFRR 3.4) — elastic_demands
text: eic Energy Identification Code scheduling area process_type type frequency restoration reserve "A47" mFRR "A51" aFRR period_start POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: An R Interface to the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API — entsoeapi
title: An R Interface to the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API — entsoeapi
text: easy wrapper around ENTSO-E API's data transform tabular format without effort.

title: Get Exchanged Volumes (aFRR 3.16, mFRR 3.17) — exchanged_volumes
text: net position informs whether given area imports exports energy. rows hold queried eic value 'ts_connecting_domain_mrid' column show export value. rows hold queried eic value 'ts_acquiring_domain_mrid' column show import value.

title: Get Exchanged Volumes (aFRR 3.16, mFRR 3.17) — exchanged_volumes
code: exchanged_volumes(   eic = NULL,   process_type = NULL,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() - lubridate::days(x = 7L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date(), tz = "CET"),   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Exchanged Volumes (aFRR 3.16, mFRR 3.17) — exchanged_volumes
text: eic Energy Identification Code area process_type type frequency restoration reserve "A51" aFRR "A60" mFRR scheduled activation "A61" mFRR direct activation period_start POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One day range limit applies period_end POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One day range limit applies tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

text: Actual aggregated net generation output (MW) consumption per market time unit per production type.

title: Get Aggregated Generation per Production Type (16.1.B&C) — gen_per_prod_type
code: gen_per_prod_type(   eic = NULL,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() - lubridate::days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date(), tz = "CET"),   gen_type = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Aggregated Generation per Production Type (16.1.B&C) — gen_per_prod_type
text: eic Energy Identification Code control area, bidding zone country period_start POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies gen_type Defaults NULL, otherwise list generation type codes asset_types table tidy_output Defaults TRUE. TRUE, flatten nested tables. security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
text: planned forced unavailability generation units expected last least one market time unit 3 years ahead. "available capacity event" means minimum available generation capacity period specified.

title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
code: outages_gen_units(   eic = NULL,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() + lubridate::days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() + lubridate::days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer),CTA|DE(TransnetBW), CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
text: planned forced unavailability production units expected last least one market time unit 3 years ahead. "available capacity event" means minimum available generation capacity period specified.

title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
code: outages_prod_units(   eic = NULL,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() + lubridate::days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date() + lubridate::days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer), CTA|DE(TransnetBW),CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform
